description_long: This is a tall, irregularly shaped stone with a rugged, natural appearance, featuring a pointed top and a broad, uneven base. The surface is mottled with shades of gray, white, and subtle green, and displays a variety of textures, from rough, jagged edges to smoother, weathered patches. Small patches of moss or lichen are visible near the base, suggesting prolonged exposure to outdoor elements. The stone has several prominent cracks and crevices, adding to its aged and organic look. Its overall form is asymmetrical, with one side appearing more vertical and narrow, while the opposite side is broader and more sloped. This stone would serve as a substantial visual and physical barrier in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A tall, rugged, asymmetrical stone with a pointed top, mottled gray surface, and patches of moss at the base. Its weathered texture and irregular shape make it a striking natural barrier.
description_view_0: A narrow, vertical profile with a pointed top and a deep central crack running down the face.
description_view_1: A broad, sloped side with a large, flat surface and visible moss at the base.
description_view_2: A jagged, vertical face with pronounced crevices and more visible moss along the bottom edge.
description_view_3: A rounded, slightly sloped side with a smoother surface and subtle color variations.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped public space
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature, partially embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and veining with natural cracks and patches of moss; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered with visible cracks, some lichen or moss growth, and signs of long-term exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Partially covered by vegetation at the base, blending naturally with planted or grassy areas, and contrasting with paved or built surfaces.
